varieties.name: Pacific Taro 🏆
varieties.origin: Pacific Islands, cultivated throughout Polynesia for centuries
varieties.traits: Large, heart-shaped leaves with purple spot in center, reddish stems at maturity, grows 1-1.5m tall
varieties.yield_notes: Moderate to high; requires 9-12 months maturity

varieties.name: Japanese Taro (Satoimo)
varieties.traits: Small, round corms with multiple cormels, compact growth habit

varieties.name: Chinese Taro (Bun Long)
varieties.traits: Dryland variety, green leaves, moderate size, drought-hardy

varieties.name: Dasheen 🏆
varieties.origin: Southeast Asia, named variety from Caribbean adaptation ('de Chine' = from China)
varieties.traits: Large heart-shaped leaves, robust plant, dryland variety

varieties.name: Eddoe (Colocasia antiquorum)
varieties.origin: Southeast Asia (China/Japan), spread to Caribbean and Africa
varieties.traits: Smaller plant than dasheen, more cormels, barrel-shaped with hairy skin

varieties.name: Giant Taro (Alocasia macrorrhiza)
varieties.origin: Southeast Asia, eastern Australia, Pacific Islands
varieties.traits: Very large plant (2-3m+), massive arrow-shaped leaves up to 1.8m long, thick edible stem
varieties.yield_notes: Very high; substantial harvest from mature plants
varieties.availability: Australian native plant nurseries, tropical plant specialists

varieties.name: Nadan Chembu (നാടൻ ചേമ്പ്) 🏆
varieties.traits: Organic backyard variety, smaller tender corms preferred, traditional homestead cultivation

varieties.name: Kochu Chembu (കൊച്ചു ചേമ്പ്) 🏆
varieties.traits: Baby taro, harvested young, very tender

varieties.name: Cheera Chembu (ചീര ചേമ്പ്) - Alocasia indica 🌱
varieties.origin: tropical monsoon climates, Indian subcontinent, 'cheera' means 'leaf vegetable'
varieties.traits: Also called Tahitian Spinach, grown primarily for leaves, two varieties (green stem and dark violet stem)
varieties.availability: Widely available in tropical monsoon climates nurseries and markets

varieties.name: Violet/Nila Chembu (വൈലറ്റ്/നീല ചേമ്പ്) - Xanthosoma violaceum
varieties.origin: Americas, introduced to tropical monsoon climates, also called Violet/Blue Tannia
varieties.traits: Purple-violet coloring, distinct from Colocasia species
varieties.availability: tropical monsoon climates nurseries, specialty suppliers

varieties.name: General Indian Native Varieties 🏆
varieties.origin: India is one of the centers of origin and domestication for taro, with naturally occurring triploid varieties showing enhanced yields
varieties.traits: High genetic diversity across India, both diploid and triploid forms
varieties.yield_notes: Variable; triploid Indian varieties show significantly better yields

varieties.name: Dasheen Type (Kerala) 🏆
varieties.origin: Southeast Asian origin, cultivated in tropical monsoon climates
varieties.traits: Large cylindrical central corm, few cormels, robust growth
varieties.availability: tropical monsoon climates agricultural markets, commercial cultivation

varieties.name: Eddoe Type (Kerala)
varieties.origin: Asian origin, grown in tropical monsoon climates
varieties.traits: Small central corm with numerous cormels, more tolerant of variable conditions
